description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels, which may serve both functional and aesthetic purposes. The block is solid and robust, with clean, sharp edges and a uniform thickness throughout. Its sides are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. The coloration is primarily a dark gray to black, with lighter gray tones visible in the grooved areas, suggesting a polished or treated stone material. This type of block is commonly used in urban environments as a barrier or obstacle, often found in plazas, sidewalks, or as part of modern landscaping.
description: A rectangular stone block with a grooved, striped top surface and smooth, solid sides in dark and light gray tones. The grooves create a visually striking pattern, typical of urban barrier or obstacle elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the grooves and the block's thickness, with grooves running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped pattern across the entire width of the block.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the block's solid, rectangular form.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained with sharp edges and no discoloration.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
